prediction_ref.short_label: Cerebras IPO prices above $22-25B with first-day pop >=30% by mid-May 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Cerebras Systems formally launches the IPO on May 4 — pricing 28M shares at $115-$125 each to raise approximately $3.5B at a $26.6B market cap at the high end (vs Series H $23B Feb 2026), would be the largest tech IPO of 2026 if priced at the high end and would clear Series H investors at the high end of the range. The S-1 disclosed $510M trailing revenue and a multi-year warrant + loan structure with OpenAI worth more than $10B; in Dec 2025 OpenAI loaned Cerebras $1B secured by warrants for over 33M shares (~12% of the float). Angel investor roster includes Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, and Adam D'Angelo. The $26.6B target sits squarely above the prediction's $22-25B band; the first-day pop magnitude is the remaining empirical question once trading opens.

The first-day pop magnitude is the only empirical leg still outstanding.
bridge.remaining_gap: First-day open-to-close pop magnitude not yet observable — pricing settles before mid-May; >=30% pop awaits the trading-day print.

prediction_ref.short_label: Agentic-AI CVE class lands as CSAI CNA sub-corpus by Q3 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Three critical AI-tooling CVEs land in the same window: n8n CVE-2026-21858 (CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated RCE via webhook content-type confusion, codenamed Ni8mare by Cyera Research Labs, patched in 1.121.0, with downstream prompt-injection scenarios via the n8n LLM-chatbot node), Flowise CVE-2026-41264 (prompt-injection-driven RCE on the CSV Agent node where a chatflow user coaxes the LLM into emitting attacker-controlled Python the Flowise server executes), and LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF (first exploited within 12 hours 31 minutes against Sysdig Threat Research honeypots). Canadian Centre for Cyber Security + Australian ASD ACSC + Censys + Orca Security publish coordinated advisories; Georgia Tech's Vibe Security Radar attributed 35 CVEs in March 2026 directly to AI coding tools with researchers estimating 5-10x undercount across the open-source ecosystem. The agentic-AI CVE category is now operationally distinguishable as its own sub-corpus through advisory-coordination patterns even though no CSAI Foundation CNA assignment lands today.

Sysdig - CVE-2026-33626 LMDeploy SSRF exploited in 12 hours
OWASP GenAI Exploit Round-up Report Q1 2026
Censys - n8n Unauthenticated RCE Ni8mare CVE-2026-21858 Advisory

bridge.narrative: The Ni8mare CVSS-10 RCE plus the Flowise prompt-injection RCE plus the LMDeploy 12h-31m exploitation latency, all multi-vendor advisory-coordinated within one week, deliver exactly the precondition the agentic-AI CVE-as-sub-corpus prediction (May 1) names: the AI-tooling CVE category is now operationally distinguishable from generic web-app CVEs by advisory shape and exploitation-latency profile.
bridge.remaining_gap: No CSAI Foundation CNA assignment for an agentic-AI CVE sub-corpus has yet been issued; advisory coordination still flows through generic CCCS / ASD / GitLab channels.

prediction_ref.short_label: Agent-skills attack surface lands MITRE-style threat sub-matrix by H2 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: n8n CVE-2026-21858 + Flowise CVE-2026-41264 + LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 cluster the no-code AI-workflow-harness attack surface into a coherent threat-class within a single window — webhook content-type confusion enabling unauthenticated RCE, prompt-injection-driven Python execution by the LLM agent itself, and SSRF in the inference-engine layer. prediction_ref.short_label: LLM-tooling + Linux-kernel CVE chain becomes dominant attack pattern by H2 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: n8n CVE-2026-21858 (CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated RCE on the underlying host via webhook content-type confusion) and the Flowise CSV Agent prompt-injection-driven RCE both land arbitrary command execution on the host OS the LLM-tooling layer runs on. The LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF first-exploitation latency of 12 hours 31 minutes against Sysdig honeypots sets the operational cadence: attackers now chain LLM-tooling vulnerabilities to OS-level execution within hours of disclosure. No specific Linux-kernel CVE chain lands today, but the prediction's pattern (LLM-tooling layer as initial access, host OS as secondary target) is empirically operative.

bridge.remaining_gap: No specific Linux-kernel CVE chain joining a published LLM-tooling RCE lands today; the host-OS landing is shown without the second-stage kernel pivot.

prediction_ref.short_label: Frontier cyber-AI export-control category emerges by Q3 2026

validation_rows.evidence_summary: The May 4-5 New York Times reporting that the Trump administration is weighing an executive order creating a federal working group of tech executives + government officials to design a pre-release vetting process for new AI models — with senior administration officials briefing Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI on the plans last week, Anthropic's Mythos cyber model (Apr 7 announcement, finding tens of thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities including a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug) cited as the immediate catalyst, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly framing a cyber 'moment of danger,' the UK NHS already ordering tech leaders to wall off hundreds of open-source GitHub repositories with a May deadline citing Mythos exposure — combined with the May 1 UK AI Safety Institute cyber evaluation that placed GPT-5.5 at 71.4% and Mythos at 68.6% on Expert-tier offensive-cyber tasks (Opus 4.7 at 48.6%) and recorded GPT-5.5 + Mythos completing AISI's 32-step 'The Last Ones' attack range end-to-end in 2 of 10 and 3 of 10 attempts respectively (AISI-estimated 20-human-hour exploit chain) form the structural force for a frontier cyber-AI export-control category. Anthropic Project Glasswing already routes Mythos access exclusively to vetted security partners — the de-facto export-control posture is operating ahead of any formal BIS / Commerce category.

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Mistral AI launches Workflows public preview — a Temporal-powered orchestration engine for enterprise multi-step AI processes, Python-defined workflows composing models + agents + external connectors with durability + observability + fault tolerance, control plane on Mistral-managed infrastructure with execution workers + data inside the customer environment, already in production at ASML, ABANCA, CMA-CGM, France Travail, La Banque Postale, and Moeve. Workflows is the EU-frontier-lab counter-shape to the May 5 Anthropic 10-agent finance launch on the Microsoft 365 paid surface, sitting between Forge (custom-model training, GTC March 2026) and Vibe (coding-agent platform). The cross-vendor agent-orchestration race is live across both AWS Bedrock + Google Vertex (via the Microsoft-OpenAI-AWS Apr 27 carry) and Mistral; AWS Bedrock + Google Vertex have not yet published parallel default MCP policy schemas in response to Microsoft Agent 365's May 1 GA.

prediction_ref.short_label: Indirect prompt injection becomes a top CVE category by Q3 2026
